title: Irvingella malayana
intro: Irvingella malayana is a plant species belonging to the family Sapindaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Malaysia and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Irvingella malayana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Irvingella malayana is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or extract, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Irvingella malayana is a species of fungus in the family Pyronemataceae. It belongs to the genus Irvingella and is classified as a member of the kingdom Fungi. Irvingella malayana is also classified as a member of the phylum Ascomycota. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Further research is needed to fully understand the taxonomic relationships within the genus Irvingella.
